observations.recommendation:
1.9 We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the Inventory
Committee conducts a physical inventory of its properties, and prepare
and submit the RPCPPE;

1.10 We also recommended that Management ensure the Municipal
Accountant and GSO Designate prepare and regularly update their
respective PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, and perform a
reconciliation of the reported PPEs per physical count, accounting, and
property records to establish the correct balances of the PPE accounts in
the financial statements;

1.11 We further recommended that Management ensure the Municipal
Accountant makes certain the complete and timely depreciation of all
depreciable properties and makes the necessary journal entries to effect
the required adjustments; and

1.12 We likewise recommended and Management agreed to ensure the
Municipal Accountant requires supporting documents such as delivery
receipts and inspection and acceptance reports prior to recording of
payables to confirm actual delivery and receipt by the Municipality.

3.7 We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the Municipal
Accountant makes certain that funds received are supported with duly
signed MOA or related documents, and makes proper recording of its
receipt and utilization.

3.8 We also recommended and Management agreed to ensure the
implementing offices make certain that the trust receipts are used solely
for their intended purpose and that funds are actually available to cover
expenditures charged against the fund.

3.9 We further recommended that Management ensure the Municipal
Accountant reconciles the erroneous entries affecting the Due to LGUs and
NGAs accounts, and effect the necessary adjustments to ensure the correct
and reliable presentation of the year-end balances in the financial
statements.

4.10 We recommended that Management ensure the Municipal Accountant
verifies the reconciling items noted in the BRS, and make the required
adjusting entries to present the correct balance of the Cash in Bank in the
books of accounts.

4.11 We also recommended that Management ensure the Office of the
Municipal Treasurer exhausts all efforts to retrieve the supporting
documents for the identified stale checks and present the same to the
Municipal Accountant for the preparation of the appropriate adjusting
entries to present fairly the Cash in Bank balance in the financial
statements.

5.7 We recommended, and the Management agreed to ensure the Municipal
Accountant makes the necessary adjustments to ensure the accurate and
reliable presentation of year-end account balances in the financial
statements.

6.13 We recommended and Management agreed to ensure:
a. The Municipal Accountant expedites the reconciliation of the identified
accounts, prepares a detailed schedule of unreconciled balances with
supporting documentation, and makes the necessary adjusting entries
to reflect the correct year-end balances of the Due to BIR, GSIS,
PhilHealth, and Pag-IBIG accounts; and
b. The Municipal Accountant designs and implements an internal control
and monitoring system to ensure that discrepancies are identified,
investigated, and resolved promptly, thereby maintaining the accuracy
of account balances.

7.11 We recommended that Management ensure:
a. The Inventory Committee conducts a physical count of inventories
held for consumption to verify their existence and prepare the
corresponding report thereon; and
b. The Municipal Accountant, upon receipt of the report, reconciles it
with accounting records and prepares the necessary adjusting entries
to reflect its actual balance in the books.

8.12 We recommended, and Management agreed to ensure the Municipal
Budget Officer submits the detailed SAAOB to the Municipal Accountant
on time to facilitate proper reconciliation.

8.13 We further recommended and Management agreed to ensure the
Municipal Accountant accurately prepares the Notes to the Financial
Statements in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-004, and verifies
the propriety and completeness of all required disclosures to support the
fair presentation of the financial statements.

9.11 We recommended that Management require the previous and current
Municipal Treasurers to produce the undeposited collections and deposit
the same with the Authorized Government Depository Bank (AGDB) and
provide an explanation why the said amount was undeposited as of
December 31, 2024, or if necessary, initiate proper proceedings.

9.12 We also recommended that Management ensure the current Municipal
Treasurer deposits intact all the collections with the AGDB daily or not
later than the next banking day as required under Section 69 of P.D. No.
1445 and Section 32 of NGAS Manual for LGUs Volume I.

10.14 We recommended that the Management ensure that the Municipal
Treasurer’s Office closely monitors the collection of current-year real
property taxes, develops and implements more effective collection
strategies to improve efficiency, and enforces available remedies under
Sections 256 to 260 of RA No. 7160 for the recovery of delinquent RPT
and SET.

10.15 We also recommended that the Management ensure the Municipal
Assessor initiates coordination with the Department of Agrarian Reform
(DAR) to verify the status and obtain updated listings of CARP-covered
properties, reconciles these with existing assessment records, and updates
the reports to include only properties subject to real property taxation.

11.15 We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the Municipal
Accountant submits the lacking documents to the Audit Team for
verification.

11.16 We also recommended and Management agreed to institute internal
control measures to ensure that future payments of CNA Incentives are
properly documented, justified, and compliant with applicable regulations.

12.12 We recommended and Management agreed to ensure:
a. The Municipal Development Council, MPDO and Municipal Engineer
as the primary implementing offices discuss, prepare, and adhere to a
timetable for project implementation of the unimplemented projects
to ensure timely completion.
b. The MPDO makes certain that all programs and projects are
procurement- and implementation-ready, in compliance with DBM-
DOF-DILG JMC No. 1, to guarantee on-time execution.
c. The Municipal Accountant, Municipal Budget Officer and MPDO
reconcile their reports regularly and establish the correct utilization
and balances of the current and continuing appropriations to ensure
the production of accurate year-end reports;
d. The Municipal Accountant makes certain that all disbursement
vouchers are supported with complete documents and submits to the
Office of the Auditor the lacking documents enumerated in Annex N;
and
e. The Municipal Accountant henceforth verifies that suspension and
resumption orders are properly approved and supported before
including them into project completion timelines; otherwise,
liquidated damages should be computed for any negative slippage
prior to payment.

13.8 We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the HRMO submits
the curriculum vitae of COS personnel, along with verifiable performance
metrics and quantifiable Accomplishment Reports, to substantiate the
payments made.

13.9 We also recommended and Management agreed to ensure the HRMO
provides a written justification for the hiring of a Finance and
Management Officer whose functions significantly overlap with those of
the Municipal Accountant to make certain that functional redundancy is
avoided and to prevent additional, potentially unnecessary, expenditures
by the Municipality.

13.10 We also recommended and Management agreed to ensure the Municipal
Accountant reviews the applicable taxes imposed on professionals and
other service providers, so that appropriate taxes are properly withheld
and remitted, as mandated under Republic Act No. 1051 and relevant tax
regulations.

14.14 We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the HRMO
maintains a complete and accurate manpower database to properly track
all JO, COS, and Casual personnel, and to confirm that all individuals are
documented and covered by valid contracts.

14.15 We also recommended and Management agreed to make certain the
HRMO have all contracts clearly specify actual assignments, wage rates,
contract duration, and other relevant details.

14.16 We further recommended and Management agreed to ensure the
Municipal Accountant verifies that all payments strictly align with the
terms of the approved contracts.

15.12 We recommended that the Management ensure the MTO:
a. Prepares and maintains accurate reports and an updated master list
of stall holders, including fees, balances and relevant details,
supported by proper documentation for effective monitoring and
decision-making; and
b. Strengthens collection efforts, enforces sanctions on delinquent stall
holders if warranted, and ensures all rentals have valid, up-to-date
lease contracts in accordance with the Municipality’s Revenue Code.

15.13 We also recommended and Management agreed that the MTO facilitates
the creation of a permanent Market Committee pursuant to the Revenue
Code of the Municipality.

16.7 We recommended and Management agreed to promptly engage with
employees to explore alternative solutions for addressing loan payment
deficiencies while ensuring compliance with the ₱5,000.00 net take-home
pay requirement under Section 56 of the GAA for FY 2024, with possible
measures such as voluntary payments to lending institutions, loan
restructuring, or extended repayment periods.

17.13 During the exit conference, Management agreed to comply with the
recommendations and confirmed that implementation will be made within the
year. The HRMO committed to requiring Contract of Service personnel to
submit their curriculum vitae, as well as performance metrics and quantifiable
accomplishment reports. The Municipal Accountant added that he will conduct
a meeting with these personnel to address tax-related concerns.

17.14 This audit observation was communicated to Management through Audit
Observation Memorandum No. 2025-15 (2024) dated May 22, 2025.

17.15 We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the Municipal
Accountant submits the lacking documents to the Audit Team for
verification.

17.16 We also recommended and Management agreed to institute internal
control measures to ensure that future payments of CNA Incentives are
properly documented, justified, and compliant with applicable regulations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to create an Inventory Committee, conduct the physical inventory of its properties, and prepare and submit the RPCPPE in accordance with Section 124, of MNGAS for LGUs Volume 1.
We also recommended the Municipal Accountant and GSO Designate to prepare and regularly update their respective PPE Ledger Cards, Real Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, and perform a reconciliation of the reported PPEs per physical count, accounting and property records to establish the correct balances of the PPE accounts in the financial statements.
Lastly, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the recognition requirement for tangible items that fall below the capitalization threshold of ₱50,000.00 as stipulated in COA Circular No. 2022-004, compute and record the depreciation expense for all depreciable properties, and make the necessary journal entries to effect the required adjustments, ensure reliability of the reported PPE balances and achieve fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended and the concerned officials and employees agreed to ensure the liquidation of their outstanding cash advances, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to henceforth, properly monitor and refrain from granting additional cash advances to those with unsettled accounts pursuant to Section 4.1.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant verify, analyze and validate the recorded accounts and identify those eligible for write-off and comply with the procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2016-005 as updated by COA Circular No. 2023-008.
We likewise recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary JEV to correct the erroneously recorded cash advances and henceforth, ensure the proper recording thereof in the books.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to ensure that funds received are supported with duly signed MOA and related documents, and to make proper recording of their receipts and utilizations.
We also recommended and the concerned officials agreed to make certain that the trust receipts are used solely for their intended purposes and that funds are actually available to cover expenditures charged against the fund.
We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconciles the negative and erroneous entries affecting the Due to LGUs and NGAs accounts, and effect the necessary adjustments to ensure the correct and reliable presentation of the year-end balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepares and submits the BRS for the old bank account held under the General Fund, verify the reconciling items noted in the BRS, and make the required adjusting entries to present the correct balance of the Cash in Bank in the books of accounts.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer exhausts all efforts to retrieve the supporting documents for the identified stale checks and present the same to the Municipal Accountant for the preparation of the appropriate adjusting entries to present fairly the Cash in Bank balance in the financial statements.
We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make the required adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to effect the necessary adjustments to correct the balances of affected accounts in the financial statements, and henceforth, ensure compliance to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 in the accounting of unutilized LDRRMF balances to ensure fair presentation of the financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to prepare the certified list of RPT and SET due and collectible based on the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) or Index Card from the Assessor’s Office and reconcile the amounts in the tax due listing generated from the MTColS at the beginning of the year to render an accurate balance of receivables.
We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to reflect the correct balance of receivables in the financial statements based on the reconciled balance provided by the Municipal Treasurer, and henceforth, recognize in the books the RPT and SET Receivables at the beginning of the year based solely on the certified list of due and collectible real property taxes for the current year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive directs the Municipal Accountant to exhaust available remedies to collect the receivables, identify the accounts eligible for write-off and comply with the procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2016-005 as updated by COA Circular No. 2023-008.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the conduct of physical count of inventories held for consumption to ascertain its existence and direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reflect its actual balance in the books.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Budget Officer to ensure the timely submission of the detailed SAAOB, and the Municipal Accountant agreed to strictly prepare the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-004 and make certain the propriety and completeness of required disclosures in order to achieve fair presentation of financial statements.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to revert to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund, the balance of LDRRMF in the STF which remained unutilized after five years, and inform the MDRRMO and Budget Officer of the transfer to properly effect the same in their respective reports. Likewise, strict observance on the proper accounting of the fund pursuant to pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 was also agreed.

We recommended and the MDRRMO and concerned Municipal Officials agreed to properly determine DRRM PPAs to be funded by the LDRRMF that is considered vital in its operations and ensure its full implementation in accordance with the guidelines set under NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-73 in order to strengthen the Municipality’s disaster risk reduction and management capability.

We also recommended and the MDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant agreed to prepare and submit the Monthly and Annual Report on Receipts and Utilization of the DRRM Fund in the prescribed format as required in NDRRMC, DBM and DILG JMC No 2013-1 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended and the Chairman of Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) agreed to henceforth procure consultancy services pursuant to the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, and the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of supporting documents in accordance with Item 9.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 before payments are made.

We recommended and Management agreed to submit a copy of the Sanggunian approved AIP and/or supplemental AIP as applicable, for the release of financial assistance.

We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to revert to the National Treasury the balance which remained unutilized and the concerned offices to submit proof of compliance to the posting and reporting requirements under Item 4.0 of LBC No. 141.

We recommended and the management agreed that the LCE together with the HRMO appoint a permanent Municipal Accountant or designate a permanent employee as Officer-in-Charge of the Municipal Accounting Office which possesses the required qualifications. Henceforth, strictly follow the guidelines provided in the Revised Omnibus Rules on Appointments and Other Human Resource Actions and Republic Act 7160 in case of designation and hiring of personnel.

We recommended and the management agreed to the following: 

The GSO Designate to conduct pre-repair evaluation/inspection, post repair evaluation/inspection and prepare waste material reports of all its government vehicles subjected to repairs and to keep an updated copy of all existing vehicles owned by the Municipality with complete data thereon; and
The OIC-Municipal Accountant to maintain a record or ledger card for all the expenses incurred regarding repairs and maintenance of every vehicle owned by the LGU to properly monitor whether the property is economically repairable.

We recommended and the management agreed to adopt the guidelines issued by DSWD particularly for the financial assistance applicable to the municipality to set forth a clear and appropriate guideline to be followed in determining the entitlement and amount of financial assistance to be granted. Henceforth, follow the provisions as set forth under Section 4(1) of PD No. 1445.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and other officials/personnel responsible:

a. comply with the prescribed general policies and procedures in the implementation of the ADM Program as provided in the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-3 and ensure that all concerned are able to abide with the prescribed processes and be accountable for the overall implementation of the program;
observations.managementAction: The Municipality did not comply with the prescribed general policies and procedures in the implementation of the ADM Program as provided in the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-3.

observations.recommendation: b. furnish the COA Office the complete set of authenticated copy of contract and other required supporting documents in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-001 in order to facilitate auditorial and legal review and adhere strictly to the aforementioned guidelines for the succeeding transactions; and
observations.managementAction: The Municipality did not furnished COA the complete set of authenticated copy of contracts and other required supporting documents.

observations.recommendation: c. observe the appropriate process of procurement as required under the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to avoid possible sanction for non-compliance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality did not observe appropriate process of procurement as required under the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the transfer of funds to other LGUs or inter-local government organizations for the implementation of certain programs/projects  of the recipient-LGU/agency be supported by an Approved Program of Work or Approved Project Expenditures/ Estimated Expenses, duly notarized MOA and/or similar document and other documentary requirements enumerated under Sections 3.1.1 and 3.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 to provide basis for monitoring, accounting and reporting of the utilization of funds.
observations.reason: The Approved Program of Work or Approved Project Expenditures/ Estimated Expenses, duly notarized MOA and/or similar document and other documentary requirements enumerated under Sections 3.1.1 and 3.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 were not yet submitted.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the transfer of funds be taken-up in the books as receivable account and that liquidation from the recipient-barangay/ inter-local government organization and/or refund of any unutilized/unexpended balance be demanded.
observations.reason: The fund transfers were not yet taken-up in the books as receivables account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Officials/ Personnel correct the aforementioned deficiencies and strictly comply to Section 89 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2003-003 by adopting the following measures:

Secure from the COA Chairman Credit advice that could serve as evidence for the liquidation of the cash advance of P300,000.00 dated December 21, 2010;
observations.reason: No credit notice was submitted to COA

observations.recommendation: Furnish the Office of the Team Leader, copy of transmittal letter for the purpose, duly received by the COA Chairman or his duly authorized representative.
observations.reason: No copy of transmittal letter was submitted to COA

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the management agreed to:
Create an Inventory Committee that will conduct physical count of all property, plant and equipment owned by the municipality and prepare and submit the required reports; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to compute and record depreciation expense and accumulated depreciation on all depreciable assets in the books of accounts.
Likewise, we recommended, and the Municipality agreed to avail of the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided under Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 to finally establish PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
ensure the liquidation of all unliquidated cash advances, and henceforth, instruct the Municipal Accountant to monitor cash advances and refrain from granting additional cash advances to those with unsettled accounts pursuant to Section 89 of PD No. 1445;
direct the Municipal Accountant to verify, analyze and validate the accounts and identify those eligible for write-off and comply with the procedures provided in COA Circular No. 2016-005; and
direct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary JEV to properly classify the cash advances.

We recommended and the management agreed that the:
Concerned personnel of the implementing offices keep a record of fund transfers receive, liquidations and balances and require them to submit immediately the liquidation documents so that balances with the source agencies could be settled; and
Municipal Accountant to:
Exert extra effort in verifying the status of transferred funds to identify unutilized balances of completed projects so that the same could be returned to the Source Agencies, if the funds will no longer be used;
Prepare the monthly Statement and Aging of Due to NGAs to ensure the proper monitoring of transferred funds and submit said reports to the Audit Team; and
Review/ verify the negative balances and effect adjusting entries in the books to eliminate the items with negative balances.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
Require the previous Municipal Treasurers to produce the undeposited collections and deposit the same with the Authorized Government Depository Bank (AGDB) and provide explanation why the said amount was undeposited as of December 31, 2021 or if necessary, initiate proper proceedings; and
Require the current OIC - Municipal Treasurer to verify and reconcile their records with the reflected amount in the FS prepared by the Municipal Accountant and to deposit intact all the collections with the AGDB daily or not later than the next banking day as required under Section 69 of PD No.  1445 and Section 32 of MNGAS for LGUs Volume I.

We recommended and the management agreed that the LCE together with the HRMO appoint a permanent Municipal Accountant or designate a permanent employee as Officer-in-Charge of the Municipal Accounting Office which possesses the required qualifications. Henceforth, strictly follow the guidelines provided in the Revised Omnibus Rules on Appointments and Other Human Resource Actions and Republic Act 7160 in case of designation and hiring of personnel.

We recommended and the management agreed to:
Re-visit the unimplemented development projects as of CY 2021 if it is still doable and necessary or it needs to be re-appropriated to other purpose(s);
Exert effort to ensure the implementation of development projects identified in the AIP to maximize the utilization of the 20% DF; and
Henceforth, ensure that all programs, projects and activities to be funded under the 20% DF are well-planned and procurement-and-implementation-ready and included in the AIP and LDIP, to enhance the delivery of services to the Municipality’s constituents.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to transfer the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF for CYs 2020-2021 to the Special Trust Fund and record the transfer of the unexpended balance for CYs 2019-2021 in the General Ledger and henceforth, to strictly observe the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We recommended and the management agreed to the following:
The GSO Designate to conduct pre-repair evaluation/inspection, post repair evaluation/inspection and prepare waste material reports of all its government vehicles subjected to repairs and to keep an updated copy of all existing vehicles owned by the Municipality with complete data thereon; and
The OIC-Municipal Accountant to maintain a record or ledger card for all the expenses incurred regarding repairs and maintenance of every vehicle owned by the LGU to properly monitor whether the property is economically repairable.

We recommended and the management agreed that the GSO-Designate insure against any insurable risk all government properties, assets and interests with the General Insurance Fund (GIF) as administered by the Government Service Insurance System (GSIS).

We recommended and the management agreed to adopt the guidelines issued by DSWD particularly for the financial assistance applicable to the municipality to set forth a clear and appropriate guideline to be followed in determining the entitlement and amount of financial assistance to be granted. Henceforth, follow the provisions as set forth under Section 4(1) of PD No. 1445.

We recommended and the management agreed to strictly enforce that proper procurement methods be followed and to withhold its corresponding taxes in order to maximize revenue that the government may earn through the withholding tax system as provided in RA 10963.

We recommended and the management agreed to assign/designate a personnel or team to accept and distribute donation in behalf of the Municipality and to prepare the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances with the required supporting documents and submit it to the Audit Team within 10 working days after the end of each quarter with proof of submission to the National Disaster Risk and Reduction Management Council (NDRRMC), through the OCD, to properly account donations received in cash or in kind.

We recommended and the management agreed to direct the:
Recipients concerned to refund the excess or unauthorized amount received;
Personnel concerned to submit a copy of the document supporting the authorized working arrangement reflecting the number of days and hours per day the HWs actually report to duty; and
Municipal Accountant to check the accuracy of the claim and completeness of the required supporting documents before payment.

We recommended and the management agreed to direct the municipal officials and employees concerned to observe strictly the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-001, by submitting copies of contracts, purchase orders, with complete details and supporting documents to the Auditor within five working days from the execution thereof.

We recommended and the management agreed to direct the GFPS of the Municipality to set the GAD Agenda or identify priority gender issues to be addressed by the LGU during the three-year term of its leadership and use this as a basis for the annual formulation of PPAs to be included in the GPB so that PAPs in the GPB be gender responsive and supportive of the gender issues of the Municipality.
We also recommended and the GAD Coordinator agreed to submit a brief summary of the reported program or project together with the GAD accomplishment report.

We recommended that the LCE:

Creates an Inventory Committee that will conduct physical count of all property, plant and equipment owned by the municipality and prepare and submit the required reports in accordance with the pertinent provisions of the GAM for LGUs while taking advantage of Commission on Audit (COA) Circular No. 2020-006 as to the One-Time Cleansing of PPE Account Balances of Government Agencies;
observations.managementAction:
Inventory Committee had been created and partial inventory was made.

This was reiterated in the current year report.

observations.recommendation: Creation of an Appraisal Committee to assist in the disposal of unserviceable properties; the person responsible to submit the IIRUP to the COA Office as required in the guidelines; and thereafter, the Accounting Office to record journal entries for the dropping of PPEs from the books of accounts once the unserviceable properties were properly disposed. (AAR CY 2019 Finding No. 1)
observations.managementAction: Municipal Appraisal Committee has been created but not functional.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the liquidation of all the unliquidated cash advances and henceforth, instruct the Municipal Accountant to monitor cash advances and refrain from granting additional cash advances to those with unsettled accounts pursuant to Section 89 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction:
Notices and demand letters has been sent to those with cash advances and some have been liquidated. Municipal Accountant is still granting cash advances even the previous advances remain unliquidated. 

This was reiterated in the current year report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 

Require the Accountant to prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers of Cash – Local Treasury accounts for every accountable officer to determine their specific accountabilities;
observations.managementAction:
Subsidiary ledgers of Cash – Local Treasury accounts for every accountable officer were prepared but the SL for general fund were not yet forwarded to the Audit Team. 

This was reiterated in the current year report.

observations.recommendation: Henceforth, direct the Acting- Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all the collections with the AGDB daily or not later than the next banking day as required under Section 69 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 8 and 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382.
observations.managementAction: LCE directed the OIC- Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all the collections daily in LBP Barotac Viejo.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE with all the Officials/employees concerned provide a justification/explanation why the amount was utilized for cash assistance to COVID-19 households and henceforth, to strictly observe the guidelines of the DILG-DBM JMC No. 01 dated March 27, 2020 in the utilization of the realigned 20% DF in order to deliver a more appropriate disaster response aid and measures for its constituents.

We recommended that the LCE require the Municipal Accountant and the personnel concerned to submit the fund utilization report with the supporting documents of the 1st tranche of fund transfer and to liquidate immediately the remaining 2nd and 3rd tranches of COVID -19 fund transfers. Henceforth, liquidate the fund transfers within the set deadline.
observations.managementAction: Fund utilization report with the supporting documents of the 1st tranche of fund transfer were not yet submitted

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE with all the Officials/employees concerned provide a justification/explanation why the utilization was not in accordance with the proposed breakdown of expenses. Likewise, the LCE require the personnel in charge to submit the resolution/guideline setting forth clear and appropriate guidelines to be followed in determining the beneficiaries and amount to be granted with regard to the cash assistance. Henceforth, to strictly comply with the approved Breakdown of Expenses provided in the MOA and project proposal as to the utilization of fund transfers.

We recommended that the LCE direct the officials/ employees concerned to submit immediately to the Office of the Auditor, the lacking documents to support the negotiated procurement of various supplies for COVID-19 relief operations and henceforth, to adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of GPPB Resolution No. 03-2020, Republic Act No. 9184 and COA Circular 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: Only the Inspection and Acceptance Reports (IAR) for the items procured were submitted.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the LCE agreed to require the concerned personnel to:

a.  Submit immediately the Monthly Report of Official Travels with the corresponding Monthly Report of Consumption of Fuel, Oil and Lubricants for each vehicle for CY 2020 to the Audit Team for verification and determination of the reasonableness of the charges made to Fuel, Oil & Lubricants
observations.managementAction: Only the Monthly Report of Official Travels of employees was submitted to the Office of the Auditor.

observations.recommendation: b. Require the designated drivers of the agency to completely and properly accomplish the trip tickets.
observations.managementAction: Driver’s Trip Tickets were attached to the DVs of payment of fuel.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LGU submit thru the personnel concerned the completely filled up payroll in the correct format, Certificate of Attendance during ECQ/daily time record/logbook and Sangguniang Bayan Resolution determining the amount to be granted to each employee taking into consideration LGUs financial capability.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer to submit on-time all the records of transactions with supporting documents to the Municipal Accountant so as to facilitate the timely preparation of the required reports; and
observations.managementAction: Records of transactions with supporting documents not submitted on time to the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the management agreed that the LCE require the concerned personnel to prepare and submit the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances quarterly with the required supporting documents to properly account the disaster relief aids and donations from local and foreign sources in accordance with COA Circular 2020-009.
observations.managementAction:
Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances was not prepared and submitted quarterly. 

This was reiterated in this current year report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct all the concerned Department Heads/Division Chiefs to submit their action plans and status of implementation on the CY 2019 AAR recommendations including that of prior years, related/connected to their departments/ divisions, and the information gathered therein be used to properly accomplish the AAPSI, and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor to properly address the deficiencies of the LGU.

Henceforth, submit to the Office of the Auditor within sixty days from the receipt of the AAR a status report on the actions taken on said audit findings and recommendations using the prescribed form under COA Memorandum No. 2014-002, Likewise furnish the DBM, the Speaker of the House of the Representative, the President of the Senate of the Philippines, the House Committee on Appropriations and the Senate Committee on Finance, either in printed form or by way of electronic document a copy of said report.

We recommended that the Accountant prepare and submit immediately the bank reconciliation statements for the various bank accounts as of December 31, 2019, and every month thereafter, to effect the necessary adjusting/correcting entries, if any. Further, explain why the 15 bank accounts maintained by the Municipality were not recorded in the books and without Bank Reconciliation Statements since its opening.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and OIC- Municipal Treasurer to submit immediately to the Office of the Auditor, the unsubmitted DVs together with its corresponding supporting documents for the period January 1 to December 31, 2019; and henceforth, to adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006.
observations.managementAction: Several DVs from previous years were already submitted to the Audit Team and the LCE directed the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to immediately submit subsequent DVs together with its corresponding attachments within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE with all the members of the Municipal Planning and Development Council:

a.Include specific and identifiable programs/projects/activities (PPAs) for the 20% DF which are attainable within the desired time frame in order to maximize the allocated funds and have an immediate impact on the socio-economic development of the Municipality; and	Include specific and identifiable programs/projects/activities (PPAs) for the 20% DF which are attainable within the desired time frame in order to maximize the allocated funds and have an immediate impact on the socio-economic development of the Municipality; and

	Strictly adhere to the provisions of the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in formulating PPAs in order to achieve the maximum benefits for its constituents,
observations.managementAction: LCE made sure that the allocations for 20% DF are fully utilized within the desired period of implementation. However, slight delays in the full implementation of certain PPAs are inevitable due to quarantine procedures and health protocols brought about the COVID-19 pandemic.

observations.recommendation: b. Strictly adhere to the provisions of the DILG and DBM JMC No. 2017-1 in formulating PPAs in order to achieve the maximum benefits for its constituents,

We also recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to submit all the DVs and complete supporting documents for expenditures charged against the 20% DF to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: Not all Disbursement Vouchers under 20% DF were submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE enforce full settlement of the disallowances by requiring the withholding of salaries or other compensation due to the person liable in satisfaction of the disallowance pursuant to the prescribed Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts as provided in Section 7.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 or request for authority from the Commission for the settlement of the disallowances by installment pursuant to COA Resolution No. 2017-021 dated November 3, 2017.
observations.managementAction: Settlements in the total amount of ₱1,736,550.00 were made and issued with Notice of Settlement of Suspension/ Disallowance/ Charge (NSSDC) for CY 2021.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant submit the disbursement vouchers and supporting documents pertaining to the payment of terminal benefits, net of the full settlement of disallowances of the retired or separated employees.

We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned officials and employees to take immediate actions on the matters listed below:

Ensure the full utilization of the allocated budget and propose plans/ programs for GAD, PWDs and the Youth to maximize the funds and benefits thereof and ensure that the relevant LGU PPAs and GAD activities will address/respond to the gender issues identified; and

Henceforth, submit to the Office of the Auditor within sixty days from the receipt of the AAR a status report on the actions taken on said audit findings and recommendations using the prescribed form under COA Memorandum No. 2014-002, Likewise furnish the DBM, the Speaker of the House of the Representative, the President of the Senate of the Philippines, the House Committee on Appropriations and the Senate Committee on Finance, either in printed form or by way of electronic document a copy of said report.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, Designated Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) and Municipal Accountant explain the excessive charges to extraordinary and miscellaneous expenses which were way beyond the allotted budget, and henceforth limit the allocation and expenditures within the ceiling prescribed under Section 325(h) of RA No. 7160.

We recommended the following:

a.) Local Chief Executive and personnel responsible to furnish immediately the COA Office copies of the Municipal Ordinance pertaining to the stall rentals and Lease Contracts in order to facilitate timely and relevant audit results;

b.)	The Officer-in-Charge on the collection of wastes to be equipped with proper personal protective gear and observe the use of separate compartment/ container with cover for specific types of wastes to ensure the containment of solid wastes while in transit.

We recommended that the LCE direct the designated General Services Officer (GSO) and/or employees concerned to observe strictly the pertinent provisions of COA Circular Nos. 2009-001 and 96-010, by submitting copy of contracts/POs to the Auditor within five (5) working days from its execution, and notifying the latter of the time and date of the scheduled deliveries within twenty-four (24) hours from the acceptance of such deliveries.
observations.managementAction:
Submission of contracts/ POs were made beyond 5 days and notification of the time and schedule of deliveries within 24 hours from acceptance of deliveries were not made. 
This was reiterated in this current year report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant clearly identify and classify the type of expense and record the amount in the appropriate account in conformity with the PPSAS, to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts in the FS.

We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Review/analyze prior year’s transactions pertaining to unidentified discrepancies, prepare a reconciliation report and take up the necessary adjustments to correct the balances of the affected accounts; and	Review/ analyze prior years’ transactions pertaining to unidentified discrepancies, prepare a reconciliation report and take up the necessary adjustments to correct the balances of the affected accounts; and
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is on the process of reconciling reciprocal accounts.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following:

a.)	Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Treasurer’s Office, demand the DVs and its supporting documents from the Office of the Mayor, and submit immediately to the COA Office in order to avoid issuance of Notices of Suspension; henceforth, all concerned personnel should adhere strictly to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 95-006; and
observations.managementAction: Some of the DVs for CYs 2014, 2015, 2016 and 2018 were already submitted in CY 2016 and 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and personnel responsible:

a.)	Provide justification why set controls were not properly observed and inappropriate procedures were repeatedly practiced;
observations.managementAction: The Municipality did not provide justification why set controls were not properly observed and inappropriate procedures were repeatedly practiced.

observations.recommendation: b.)	Perform duties and responsibilities with due diligence in implementing strong and sound internal control through detailed verification of supporting documents, compliance of the appropriate procurement process, and reasonableness of amount and quantity requested, prior to the processing and approval of documents for disbursements; and

c.)	Revisit the installed internal control system of the Municipality and all the coordinate methods and measures adopted within, and make necessary modifications in order to safeguard the assets, check the accuracy and reliability of the accounting data; and adhere strictly to the prescribed laws, rules and regulations to minimize if not avoid suspensions or disallowances of transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

a.)	Direct the submission of justification why disbursements were paid through reimbursements instead of direct payment resulting to non-conformity with the appropriate procurement methods; and
observations.managementAction:
The LCE has directed the concerned personnel to submit justification.

This was reiterated in the current year report.

observations.recommendation: b.)	Strictly enforce that all payments must be made by check directly to the suppliers/ dealers/creditors in the disbursement of government funds, except for petty disbursements out of the petty cash fund, and instruct the Municipal Accountant to withhold its corresponding taxes in order to avoid delinquencies and revenue losses on the part of the government, in adherence to the BIR regulations.
observations.managementAction: Reimbursements were discouraged and only small petty expenses were allowed to be reimbursed.

observations.recommendation: b.)	OIC-Municipal Treasurer and personnel responsible to maximize the collection of market stall rentals to attain their targeted revenue collections; and
observations.managementAction: Meetings were conducted with market stall holders and businessmen to increase collections.

observations.recommendation: c.)	Municipal Accountant to record all the revenues earned in the books of accounts to effect the correct balances in the Financial Statements

We recommended the following:

a.) The Municipality to require the strict implementation of waste segregation at source through coordination with the Barangay Officials  on the campaign of proper segregation and disposal of wastes; and

b.)	The Officer-in-Charge on the collection of wastes to be equipped with proper personal protective gear and observe the use of separate compartment/ container with cover for specific types of wastes to ensure the containment of solid wastes while in transit.

We recommended that the LCE direct the concerned officials and employees to:

a.)	Propose program and budget for the implementation of Information System which is timely and relevant especially in a computerized generation, and closely monitor the implementation in order to avoid delay in the completion thereof; and
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has already partnered with different government agencies for the modernization of our information system especially with the management and organization of pertinent data by our department offices in order to expedite transactions for convenience and efficacy

observations.recommendation: b.)	Hasten the procurement of the needed services and ICT equipment in order to ensure the completion of the project as planned.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has procured additional computer units and several IT implements and already conducted necessary trainings for the employees responsible for managing the systems.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE), other local officials/ personnel responsible and the Local School Board focus the allocation of the SEF in providing adequate facilities and resources in the delivery of quality education to public elementary and secondary schools in the Municipality.

We also recommended the implementation of the Early Childhood Care and Development programs and suggest other programs/ activities relevant to the provisions of Section 272 of RA No. 7160 to fully utilize the allocated fund and maximize the benefits for the intended recipients and adhere strictly to the provisions of DepEd, DBM and DILG JC. No. 1, series of 2017 on the utilization of the SEF to avoid possible suspensions/disallowances of disbursements.
We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant and OIC-Municipal Treasurer to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.
observations.managementAction: The complete supporting documents were not yet submitted to COA.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that the PEI received by the municipal employees be refunded.
observations.managementAction: Notice of Disallowance was already issued and partial settlements were made by the employees.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit the CRAAF to the Auditor not later than the fifth (5th) day of the ensuing month pursuant to Sections 50 and 51 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly observe the budgetary constraints and adhere to the provisions of Section 325(a) of the Local Government Code of 1991 and Local Budget Circular No. 98 dated October 14, 2011.
We also recommended that the Municipality no longer provide for additional PS cost until such time that the PS cap is observed.

We recommended that the  Municipality  insure  all  its  insurable  properties/ interests only  with  the  GSIS  as hedge against the risks of damages or losses from casualty/ catastrophes to ensure early recovery and counteract development setbacks.
observations.managementAction:
Not all insurable properties were insured.

This was reiterated in the current year report.

observations.managementAction: A committee was created to commence the investigation of the whereabouts of the alleged excess materials. In case any irregularity was found, an order was issued to those liable to pay or refund the amount  involved.
